chunk_summary: This section addresses delayed texture progression in a child with neurodisability, focusing on caregiver fear, pain, skill mismatch, and feeding dynamics, with structured strategies for safe texture advancement.
learning_objectives: Identify and address pain-related drivers (e.g., constipation, reflux) in feeding difficulties.
Apply structured texture progression using IDDSI guidelines to support developmental feeding skills.
Recognize when to refer to a feeding therapist or SLP for persistent issues or caregiver distress.
key_facts: The child is 26 months old with global developmental delay and eats only smooth purées.
Mealtimes last 60 minutes, and weight is falling across centiles.
Pain drivers like constipation or reflux should be screened and addressed first.
Use IDDSI terms for structured texture progression, changing one variable at a time (e.g., particle size or viscosity).
Referral to a feeding therapist or SLP is recommended if weight faltering persists or caregiver fear remains high.
tags_mesh: Feeding and Eating Disorders of Childhood
reference_quote: Use a structured texture ladder using IDDSI terms so the whole team speaks the same diet code.
chunk_heading: NEURODISABILITY AND FEEDING SUPPORT;CASE-BASED APPLICATION DELAYED TEXTURE PROGRESSION WITH CAREGIVER FEAR;
subtopic: CASE-BASED APPLICATION DELAYED TEXTURE PROGRESSION WITH CAREGIVER FEAR
